mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 8.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses the 8.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above two percent annualized during inflationary periods confirms supply-side resilience, preventing stagflation fears and yielding positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above two percent coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in greater than 50 percent of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 215.0 signals accelerating price pressures that force immediate risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of instances following CPIAUCSL readings above 215.0.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, mechanically boosting corporate revenues and producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day SPX returns over a five-year sample with a correlation coefficient below negative 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer transmission, causing earnings downgrades and negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns with statistical significance at the ten percent level.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding one point five percent signals severe supply chain contraction and capacity utilization drops, triggering bearish equity repricing over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances following INDPRO declines exceeding one point five percent.
